narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, father of Ali ibn Ibrahim, identified from the preceding narrator
narrators.known_identity: Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, one of the earliest transmitters of Kufan hadith to Qum

narrators.ambiguity_note: Most likely al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Fadhdhal (d. 224 AH), prominent Waqifi/Fathite transmitter whose narrations are accepted by Shia scholars
narrators.known_identity: Al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Fadhdhal al-Taymi al-Kufi

narrators.ambiguity_note: Ali ibn Uqba ibn Qais ibn Sim'an, a Kufan transmitter from the circle of Imam al-Sadiq and Imam al-Kazim
narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Uqba, Kufan companion of Imam al-Sadiq and al-Kazim

narrators.ambiguity_note: The text explicitly clarifies 'meaning the First', distinguishing Imam Musa al-Kazim (AS) from later Imams also called Abu al-Hasan
narrators.known_identity: Imam Musa ibn Ja'far al-Kazim (AS), the seventh Imam (d. 183 AH)
narrators.name_ar: أَبُو الْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ يَعْنِي الْأَوَّلَ
narrators.name_en: Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him), meaning the First

key_phrases.phrase_en: in its appropriate place (i.e., to proper recipients)

bn.تَامَّة: সম্পূর্ণ — যাকাত সঠিক পরিমাণে প্রদান করার উপর জোর
bn.زَكَاة: যাকাত — সম্পদের উপর বাধ্যতামূলক কর, ইসলামের পাঁচটি স্তম্ভের একটি
bn.مَوْضِعِهَا: তার উপযুক্ত স্থান — কুরআন 9:60-এ উল্লিখিত আট ধরনের যাকাত প্রাপকদের উল্লেখ

de.تَامَّة: Vollständig — betont, dass die Zakat im richtigen Betrag gezahlt werden muss
de.زَكَاة: Zakat — obligatorische Vermögenssteuer, einer der fünf Pfeiler des Islam
de.مَوْضِعِهَا: Der angemessene Platz — bezieht sich auf die acht Kategorien von Zakat-Empfängern in Sure 9:60

en.تَامَّة: Complete, full — emphasizing that the zakat must be paid in the correct amount without reduction
en.زَكَاة: Zakat — the obligatory alms-tax on wealth, one of the five pillars of Islam; here emphasizing 'complete' zakat (tammah)
en.مَوْضِعِهَا: Its appropriate place — refers to the eight categories of zakat recipients mentioned in Quran 9:60

es.تَامَّة: Completo, íntegro — enfatizando que el zakat debe pagarse en la cantidad correcta
es.زَكَاة: Zakat — impuesto obligatorio sobre la riqueza, uno de los cinco pilares del Islam
es.مَوْضِعِهَا: Su lugar apropiado — se refiere a las ocho categorías de receptores de zakat mencionadas en el Corán 9:60

fa.تَامَّة: کامل — تأکید بر ادای زکات به مقدار صحیح
fa.زَکَات: زکات — مالیات اجباری بر ثروت، یکی از ارکان پنج‌گانه اسلام
fa.مَوْضِعِهَا: جای مناسبش — اشاره به هشت دسته مستحقان زکات در قرآن 9:60

fr.تَامَّة: Complète, totale — soulignant que la zakat doit être payée dans le montant correct
fr.زَكَاة: Zakat — impôt obligatoire sur la richesse, l'un des cinq piliers de l'Islam
fr.مَوْضِعِهَا: Sa place appropriée — fait référence aux huit catégories de bénéficiaires de la zakat mentionnées dans le Coran 9:60

id.تَامَّة: Sempurna, penuh — menekankan bahwa zakat harus dibayar dalam jumlah yang benar
id.زَكَاة: Zakat — pajak wajib atas kekayaan, salah satu dari lima rukun Islam
id.مَوْضِعِهَا: Tempatnya yang tepat — mengacu pada delapan golongan penerima zakat dalam Quran 9:60

ru.تَامَّة: Полный — подчёркивает, что закят должен быть выплачен в правильном размере
ru.زَكَاة: Закят — обязательная пошлина с имущества, один из пяти столпов ислама
ru.مَوْضِعِهَا: Надлежащее место — относится к восьми категориям получателей закята в Коране 9:60

tr.تَامَّة: Eksiksiz, tam — zekatın doğru miktarda ödenmesi gerektiğini vurgular
tr.زَكَاة: Zekat — servetten alınan zorunlu alms vergisi, İslam'ın beş şartından biri
tr.مَوْضِعِهَا: Uygun yeri — Kuran 9:60'ta geçen sekiz zekat alıcı kategorisine atıfta bulunur

ur.تَامَّۃ: مکمل، پوری — زکوٰة کا صحیح مقدار میں ادا کیا جانا
ur.زَکَاة: زکوٰة — مال پر واجب ٹیکس، اسلام کے پانچ ارکان میں سے ایک
ur.مَوْضِعِہَا: اس کا مناسب مقام — قرآن 9:60 میں مذکور آٹھ مستحقین کی طرف اشارہ

zh.تَامَّة: 完整——强调天课必须按正确数额缴纳
zh.زَكَاة: 天课——对财富征收的强制税，伊斯兰五功之一
zh.مَوْضِعِهَا: 适当之处——指《古兰经》9:60中提到的八类天课受益人

seo_questions.bn: সম্পূর্ণ যাকাত আদায় কি কিয়ামতের দিন সম্পদ সম্পর্কে প্রশ্ন থেকে মুক্তি দেয়?
seo_questions.de: Schützt die vollständige Zahlung der Zakat davor, am Tag des Gerichts nach der Herkunft des Vermögens gefragt zu werden?
seo_questions.en: Does paying complete zakat absolve a person from being questioned about their wealth on the Day of Judgment?
seo_questions.es: ¿Pagar el zakat completo exime de ser preguntado sobre la riqueza en el Día del Juicio?
seo_questions.fa: آیا پرداخت زکات کامل موجب می‌شود که در قیامت از منبع ثروت سوال نشود؟
seo_questions.fr: Le paiement complet de la zakat protège-t-il d'être interrogé sur sa richesse au Jour du Jugement ?
seo_questions.id: Apakah membayar zakat secara sempurna menghindarkan seseorang dari pertanyaan tentang hartanya di Hari Kiamat?
seo_questions.ru: Освобождает ли полная выплата закята от вопросов об источнике богатства в День Суда?
seo_questions.tr: Tam zekat ödemek, kıyamet gününde maldan hesap sorulmasını önler mi?
seo_questions.ur: کیا مکمل زکوٰة ادا کرنے سے قیامت کے دن مال کے بارے میں سوال نہیں ہوگا؟
seo_questions.zh: 完整缴纳天课是否可以免除复生日关于财富来源的追问？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths about zakat purifying wealth and the obligation to pay it correctly, including what constitutes proper distribution among the eight categories of recipients.
Traditions about the accountability of wealth on the Day of Judgment and how righteous financial conduct exempts one from interrogation about wealth sources.
Hadiths warning against withholding or delaying zakat, and the severe consequences for those who do not pay it.

summaries.bn: ইমাম মুসা আল-কাজিম (আ.) ঘোষণা করেন যে সম্পূর্ণভাবে যাকাত আদায় — পরিমাণ এবং সঠিক বিতরণ উভয়ই — কিয়ামতের দিন একজন ব্যক্তিকে তার সম্পদের উৎস সম্পর্কে জবাবদিহিতা থেকে মুক্তি দেয়। এই হাদিসটি আর্থিক ধর্মীয় বাধ্যবাধকতা পালনের পরিণতি সম্পর্কে প্রাথমিক ইসলামী ফিকহের প্রসঙ্গে বর্ণিত হয়েছে। যাকাত সম্পদ পবিত্র করে এবং এর প্রদান সম্পদ অর্জনের বৈধতার প্রশ্ন দূর করে।
summaries.de: Imam Musa al-Kazim (Friede sei mit ihm) erklärt, dass die vollständige Erfüllung der Zakat — sowohl in Bezug auf den Betrag als auch auf die ordnungsgemäße Verteilung — eine Person von der Rechenschaft über die Quelle ihres Vermögens am Tag des Gerichts befreit. Dieser Hadith wurde im Kontext des frühen islamischen Fiqh über die Folgen der Erfüllung finanzieller religiöser Pflichten überliefert. Die Zakat reinigt das Vermögen, und ihre Zahlung beseitigt die Frage nach der Rechtmäßigkeit des Erwerbs.
summaries.en: Imam Musa al-Kazim (peace be upon him) declares that complete fulfillment of zakat — both in amount and in proper distribution — absolves a person from accountability regarding the source of their wealth on the Day of Judgment. This hadith was transmitted in the context of early Islamic jurisprudence concerning the consequences of fulfilling financial religious obligations. The teaching addresses concerns about the legitimacy of wealth accumulation, assuring that diligent payment of zakat purifies one's wealth entirely.
summaries.es: El Imam Musa al-Kazim (la paz sea con él) declara que el cumplimiento completo del zakat — tanto en cantidad como en distribución apropiada — absuelve a una persona de la responsabilidad sobre el origen de su riqueza en el Día del Juicio. Este hadith fue transmitido en el contexto del fiqh islámico temprano sobre las consecuencias del cumplimiento de las obligaciones financieras religiosas. El zakat purifica la riqueza y su pago elimina la cuestión sobre la legitimidad de su adquisición.
summaries.fa: امام موسی کاظم علیه السلام اعلام می‌کنند که ادای کامل زکات — هم از نظر مقدار و هم از نظر توزیع صحیح — شخص را از پاسخگویی درباره منبع ثروتش در روز قیامت معاف می‌کند. این حدیث در زمینه فقه اسلامی اولیه درباره پیامدهای انجام تکالیف مالی دینی منتقل شده است. زکات مال را پاک می‌کند و پرداخت آن پرسش از مشروعیت کسب ثروت را برطرف می‌سازد.
summaries.fr: L'Imam Musa al-Kazim (que la paix soit sur lui) déclare que l'accomplissement complet de la zakat — tant en montant qu'en distribution appropriée — absout une personne de toute responsabilité concernant la source de sa richesse au Jour du Jugement. Ce hadith a été transmis dans le contexte du fiqh islamique primitif sur les conséquences de l'accomplissement des obligations financières religieuses. La zakat purifie la richesse et son paiement élimine la question de la légitimité de son acquisition.
summaries.id: Imam Musa al-Kazim (a.s.) menyatakan bahwa menunaikan zakat secara sempurna — baik dalam jumlah maupun distribusi yang tepat — membebaskan seseorang dari pertanggungjawaban tentang sumber kekayaannya di Hari Kiamat. Hadis ini diriwayatkan dalam konteks fiqih Islam awal mengenai konsekuensi memenuhi kewajiban finansial agama. Zakat menyucikan harta dan dengan membayarnya, pertanyaan tentang legitimasi perolehan kekayaan gugur.
summaries.ru: Имам Муса аль-Казим (мир ему) заявляет, что полное исполнение обязанности закята — как по сумме, так и по надлежащему распределению — освобождает человека от ответственности за источник его состояния в День Суда. Этот хадис был передан в контексте раннего исламского фикха о последствиях исполнения финансовых религиозных обязанностей. Закят очищает имущество, а его уплата устраняет вопрос о законности его приобретения.
summaries.tr: İmam Musa el-Kazım (a.s.), zekatın eksiksiz ve uygun yerlere ödenmesinin, kıyamette servetin kaynağı hakkında hesap sorulmaktan kurtardığını beyan etmektedir. Bu hadis, mali dini yükümlülüklerin yerine getirilmesinin sonuçlarına dair erken İslam fıkhı bağlamında rivayet edilmiştir. Zekat ödeme, servetin tamlığını temizler ve zenginliğin nasıl elde edildiği sorusunu ortadan kaldırır.
summaries.ur: امام موسیٰ کاظم علیہ السلام نے فرمایا کہ زکوٰة کو مکمل طور پر اور اس کے مناسب مقام پر ادا کرنا قیامت کے دن اپنے مال کے ذرائع کمائی کے بارے میں محاسبے سے آزادی دیتا ہے۔ یہ روایت مالی مذہبی فریضے کو ادا کرنے کے اثرات کے بارے میں ابتدائی اسلامی فقہ کے سیاق میں منتقل ہوئی۔ زکوٰة مال کو پاک کرتی ہے اور اس کی ادائیگی سے دولت جمع کرنے کی جائزیت کا سوال ختم ہو جاتا ہے۔
summaries.zh: 伊玛目穆萨·卡兹姆（愿主赐他平安）宣布，完整履行天课义务——无论是数量还是正确分配——将使一个人在复生日免于为财富来源负责。这段圣训在早期伊斯兰法学关于履行财务宗教义务的后果的背景下传述。天课净化财富，缴纳天课消除了对财富获取合法性的质疑。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن أو موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/15)

parts.text: أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) يَعْنِي الْأَوَّلَ

text: سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ مَنْ أَخْرَجَ زَكَاةَ مَالِهِ تَامَّةً فَوَضَعَهَا فِي مَوْضِعِهَا لَمْ يُسْأَلْ مِنْ أَيْنَ اكْتَسَبَ مَالَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Fazzal, from Ali Bin Uqba,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al Hassanasws, meaning the 1st, said, ‘I heard himasws saying: ‘The one who takes out the complete Zakāt of his wealth, so he places it in its (appropriate) place, would not be asked (on the Day of Judgement) from where he earned his wealth’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn Faddal from Ali ibn ‘Uqbah from abu al-Hassan, the first, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, who has said the following: “If one pays, completely, the amount of zakat which is due on his wealth to the proper recipient, he is not asked how he has earned his wealth.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/16)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ سَيُطَوَّقُونَ ما بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ قَالَ مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ مَنَعَ مِنْ زَكَاةِ مَالِهِ شَيْئاً إِلَّا جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لَهُ ذَلِكَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ثُعْبَاناً مِنْ نَارٍ يُطَوَّقُ فِي عُنُقِهِ يَنْهَشُ مِنْ لَحْمِهِ حَتَّى يَفْرُغَ مِنَ الْحِسَابِ وَ هُوَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ سَيُطَوَّقُونَ ما بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ قَالَ مَا بَخِلُوا بِهِ مِنَ الزَّكَاةِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Ibn Mihran, from Ibn Muskan, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’farasws about the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [3:180] they shall have what they were niggardly of to cleave to their necks on the Day of Judgement. Heasws said: ‘There is none from a servant who prevents anything from the Zakāt of his wealth except that Allahazwj, on the Day of Judgement, would Make that to be a cloth of Fire in his neck, eroding his flesh, until he is free from the Reckoning; and these are the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [3:180] they shall have what they were niggardly of to cleave to their necks on the Day of Judgement, what they were niggardly with, from the Zakāt’.
en.sarwar: 10. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from ibn Mehran from ibn Muskan from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the words of Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, ‘. . . whatever they are avaricious about will be tied to their necks on the Day of Judgment. . .’ [3:180]. The Imam said, ‘O abu Muhammad, if one refuses to pay zakat, Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, on the Day of Judgment will make a serpent of fire to curl around his neck and bite his flesh until He completes His judging all people.’ He then said, ‘The words of Allah, “Whatever they are avaricious about will be tied to their necks” are a reference to their avarice in paying zakat.’””

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/16)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَنْ مَنَعَ الزَّكَاةَ سَأَلَ الرَّجْعَةَ عِنْدَ الْمَوْتِ وَ هُوَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ رَبِّ ارْجِعُونِ. لَعَلِّي أَعْمَلُ صالِحاً فِيما تَرَكْتُ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Husayn, from Wuheyb Bin Hafs, from Abu Baseer who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘The one who prevents the Zakāt would ask to be return during death, and these are the Words of the Mighty and Majestic [23:99] Lord, send me back [23:100] Perhaps I may do righteous deeds which I had neglected’.
en.sarwar: 11. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ali ibn al-Husayn from Wahab ibn Hafs from abu Basir who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘One who refuses to pay zakat, on the time of his death asks to be returned to life again and this is referred to in the words of Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, “. . . Lord, allow me to go back so I can do good deeds in what I have left. . (23:99-100)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/16)
text: صَلَاةٌ مَكْتُوبَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِنْ عِشْرِينَ حَجَّةً وَ حَجَّةٌ خَيْرٌ مِنْ بَيْتٍ مَمْلُوءٍ ذَهَباً يُنْفِقُهُ فِي بِرٍّ حَتَّى يَنْفَدَ

قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ وَ لَا أَفْلَحَ مَنْ ضَيَّعَ عِشْرِينَ بَيْتاً مِنْ ذَهَبٍ بِخَمْسَةٍ وَ عِشْرِينَ دِرْهَماً فَقُلْتُ وَ مَا مَعْنَى خَمْسَةٍ وَ عِشْرِينَ دِرْهَماً قَالَ مَنْ مَنَعَ الزَّكَاةَ وُقِفَتْ صَلَاتُهُ حَتَّى يُزَكِّيَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ali Bin Hassan, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘A Prescribed Salāt is better than twenty Hajjs, and a Hajj is better than a house filled with gold spent in righteousness until it depletes’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘Then heasws said: ‘And there is no success for the one who wastes twenty houses of gold by twenty five Dirhams’. So I said, ‘What is the meaning of ‘twenty five Dirhams’?’ Heasws said: ‘The one who prevents the Zakāt, his Salāt would be paused until he purifies it (pays Zakāt)’.
en.sarwar: 12. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ali ibn Hassa’n from certain individuals of his people who have said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that performing an obligatory Salah (prayer) is more excellent than twenty Hajj and one Hajj is more excellent than giving as charity one houseful of gold.’ The narrator has said that the Imam then said, ‘There is no salvation for one who loses twenty housefuls of gold for only (because of not paying) twenty- five dirham.’ I (the narrator) then asked, ‘What is the meaning of twenty- five dirham?’ The Imam replied, ‘One who fails to pay zakat his Salah (prayer) is withheld until he pays zakat which is due on his wealth.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/16)
text: قَالَ مَلْعُونٌ مَلْعُونٌ مَالٌ لَا يُزَكَّى .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from Haroun Bin Muslim, from Mas’ada Bin Sadaqa,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Accursed! Accursed is the wealth not being purified (Zakāt not being paid from it)’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from Harun ibn Muslim from Mas’adah ibn Sadaqah who has said the following: “Abu ’Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that condemned, and condemned indeed is (a property or) one who does not pay zakat.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/16)
text: مَنْ مَنَعَ قِيرَاطاً مِنَ الزَّكَاةِ فَلْيَمُتْ إِنْ شَاءَ يَهُودِيّاً أَوْ نَصْرَانِيّاً .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from the one who mentioned it, from Hafs Bin Umar, from Salim, from Abu Baseer,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The one who prevents a carat of Zakāt, so let him die, if he so desires to as a Jew, or a Christian’.
en.sarwar: 14. Abu Ali al-Ash’ariy has narrated from those whom he has mentioned from Hafs ibn ‘Umar from Salim from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that one (Muslim) who does not pay zakat dies as a Jew or a Christian; (he is given the chance to choose).”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/17)
text: حَدَّثَنِي مَنْ سَمِعَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَا ضَاعَ مَالٌ فِي بَرٍّ وَ لَا بَحْرٍ إِلَّا بِتَضْيِيعِ الزَّكَاةِ وَ لَا يُصَادُ مِنَ الطَّيْرِ إِلَّا مَا ضَيَّعَ تَسْبِيحَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hassan, from Ali Bin Al Numan, from Is’haq who said,

‘It was narrated to me by the one who heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘Neither is wealth destroyed in a land nor a sea unless the Zakāt was wasted (not taken out), and none from a bird gets hunted until it wastes its Glorification (not doing it)’.
en.sarwar: 15. Ahmad ibn Muhammad has narrated from Ali ibn al-Hassan from Ali ibn al-Nu‘man from Ishaq who has said the following: “Narrated to me one who had heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘A property is not lost, on land or in the sea, unless payment of its zakat is withheld. A bird does not become victim of hunting unless it forgets glorifying Allah.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/17)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَانِعُ الزَّكَاةِ يُطَوَّقُ بِحَيَّةٍ قَرْعَاءَ وَ تَأْكُلُ مِنْ دِمَاغِهِ وَ ذَلِكَ قَوْلُهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ سَيُطَوَّقُونَ ما بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ.

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Al iBin Uqba, from Ayoub Bin Rashid who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘The preventer of the Zakāt would be collared with a bald snake, and it would eat from his brain; and that is in the Words of the Mighty and Majestic [3:180] they shall have what they were niggardly of to cleave to their necks on the Day of Judgement’.
en.sarwar: 16. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from Ali ibn ‘Uqbah from Ayyub ibn Rashid who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘One who refuses to pay zakat will have a bald serpent curled around him. It will feed on his brain. This is mentioned in the words of Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, “. . . whatever they are avaricious about will be tied to their necks on the Day of Judgment. . . ’”” [3:180]

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/17)
text: وَجَدْنَا فِي كِتَابِ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) إِذَا مُنِعَتِ الزَّكَاةُ مَنَعَتِ الْأَرْضُ بَرَكَاتِهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Malik Bin Atiyya, from Abu Hamza,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘Weasws found in the Book of Aliasws: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘When the Zakāt is prevented, the earth is Prevented (from) its Blessings’.
en.sarwar: 17. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from Malik ibn ‘Atiyyah from abu Hamzah who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘We have found in the book of Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, that the Messenger of Allah said, ‘When paying zakat is refused, the land withholds her blessings.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/17)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ مَا مِنْ طَيْرٍ يُصَادُ إِلَّا بِتَرْكِهِ التَّسْبِيحَ وَ مَا مِنْ مَالٍ يُصَابُ إِلَّا بِتَرْكِ الزَّكَاةِ .

en.hubeali: Abu Abdullah Al Aasimy, from Ali Bin Al Hassan Al Maysami, from Ali Bin Asbaat, from his father Asbaat Bin Salim, from Salim, a slave of Aban who said,
‘I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘There is none from a bird which gets hunted except by neglecting its Glorification, and there is none from a wealth getting wasted except by neglecting the Zakāt’.
en.sarwar: 18. Abu ‘Abd Allah al-‘Asemiy has narrated from Ali ibn al-Hassan al-Mithamiy from Ali ibn Asbat from his father, Asbat ibn Salim from Salim Mawla Aban who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘A bird does not become victim of hunting unless it forgets Tasbih (glorifying Allah). A property is not lost unless zakat which is due on it is withheld.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/17)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا مِنْ ذِي مَالٍ ذَهَبٍ أَوْ فِضَّةٍ يَمْنَعُ زَكَاةَ مَالِهِ إِلَّا حَبَسَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ بِقَاعٍ قَرْقَرٍ وَ سَلَّطَ عَلَيْهِ شُجَاعاً أَقْرَعَ يُرِيدُهُ وَ هُوَ يَحِيدُ عَنْهُ فَإِذَا رَأَى أَنَّهُ لَا مَخْلَصَ لَهُ مِنْهُ أَمْكَنَهُ مِنْ يَدِهِ فَقَضِمَهَا كَمَا يُقْضَمُ الْفُجْلُ

ثُمَّ يَصِيرُ طَوْقاً فِي عُنُقِهِ وَ ذَلِكَ قَوْلُ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ سَيُطَوَّقُونَ ما بَخِلُوا بِهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ وَ مَا مِنْ ذِي مَالٍ إِبِلٍ أَوْ غَنَمٍ أَوْ بَقَرٍ يَمْنَعُ زَكَاةَ مَالِهِ إِلَّا حَبَسَهُ اللَّهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ بِقَاعٍ قَرْقَرٍ يَطَؤُهُ كُلُّ ذَاتِ ظِلْفٍ بِظِلْفِهَا وَ يَنْهَشُهُ كُلُّ ذَاتِ نَابٍ بِنَابِهَا وَ مَا مِنْ ذِي مَالٍ نَخْلٍ أَوْ كَرْمٍ أَوْ زَرْعٍ يَمْنَعُ زَكَاتَهَا إِلَّا طَوَّقَهُ اللَّهُ رَيْعَةَ أَرْضِهِ إِلَى سَبْعِ أَرَضِينَ إِلَى يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Khalaf Bin Hammad, from Hareyz who said,

‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘There is none from the ones with wealth, be it gold or silver, who prevents the Zakāt of his wealth, except that Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic would Reckon him on the Day of Judgement in a pebbly spot, and a marsh serpent to overcome him, wanting him, and he would depart from it. So when it sees that he cannot finish himself from it, it would overpower him from his hand, so it would nibble him just as it would nibble a garden radish.

Then it would become a collar in his neck, and these are the Words of Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic [3:180] they shall have what they were niggardly of to cleave to their necks on the Day of Judgement. And there is none from the ones with wealth, either camels or sheep or cows, who prevents the Zakāt of his wealth, except that Allahazwj would Reckon him on the Day of Judgement upon a pebbly spot, wherein he would be trampled by everything with a hoof by its hooves, and being torn by everything with a claw by its claws; and there is none from the ones with wealth, either a palm tree, or a vineyard, or a plantation who prevents its Zakāt, except that Allahazwj would Collar him a pasture, or a land up to the seventh earth, up to the Day of Judgement’.
en.sarwar: 19. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Muhammad ibn Khalid from Khalaf ibn Hammad from Hariz who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Any wealthy person owning gold and silver who refuses to pay zakat, Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, on the Day of Judgment will keep him in captivity in a flat land. He (Allah) will send a bald serpent who will chase him and he will flee until he finds it impossible to escape. He (the person) will then allow the serpent to get his hand and it will bite it like a turnip. It will then curl around his neck. This is referred to in the words of Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, “. . . whatever they are avaricious about will be tied to their necks on the Day of Judgment . . .” [3:180] Every owner of camels, sheep or cows who refuses to pay zakat will be held in Allah’s captivity on the Day of Judgment, in a flat land where every hooved-animal will walk on him and every sharp-toothed animal will bite him. Every owner of palm trees, vineyards or plantation who refuses to pay zakat will be made by Allah to have that land up to seven levels tied around his neck up to the Day of Judgment.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/18)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مَا حَبَسَ عَبْدٌ زَكَاةً فَزَادَتْ فِي مَالِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, from hisasws fatherasws having said: ‘Rasool-Allahsaww said: ‘A servant would not withhold Zakāt, so there would be an increase in his wealth’.
en.sarwar: 20. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father, from af-Nawfafiy from al-Sakuniy from abu ’Abd Allah from his father, ‘Alayhim al- Salam, who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah has said, ‘If a person withholds payment of zakat due on his wealth, his wealth does not increase.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6/18)
text: مَنْ مَنَعَ حَقّاً لِلَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَنْفَقَ فِي بَاطِلٍ مِثْلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hisham Bin Al Hakam,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who prevents a Right of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would spend double the like of it in falsehood (the wrong way)’.
en.sarwar: 21. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hisham ibn al-Hakam from who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that one who refuses to pay the right of Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, spends double as much for falsehood.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/18)
text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى يَبْعَثُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ نَاساً مِنْ قُبُورِهِمْ مَشْدُودَةً أَيْدِيهِمْ إِلَى أَعْنَاقِهِمْ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ أَنْ يَتَنَاوَلُوا بِهَا قِيسَ أَنْمُلَةٍ مَعَهُمْ مَلَائِكَةٌ يُعَيِّرُونَهُمْ تَعْيِيراً شَدِيداً يَقُولُونَ هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ مَنَعُوا خَيْراً قَلِيلًا مِنْ خَيْرٍ كَثِيرٍ هَؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ أَعْطَاهُمُ اللَّهُ فَمَنَعُوا حَقَّ اللَّهِ فِي أَمْوَالِهِمْ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ayoub Bin Nuh, from Ibn Sinan, from Abu Al Jaroud,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Blessed and High would be Resurrecting a group of people from their graves on the Day of Judgement, with their hands tied tight upon their necks, not being able to take anything with them from even a distance of a fingertip. With them would be Angels taunting them with severe taunts, saying: ‘These ones are those who prevented a little good from a lot of good! These are the ones whom Allah<sup>azwj</sup> had Given, but they prevented a Right of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> in their wealth’.
en.sarwar: 22. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ayyub ibn Nuh from ibn Sinan from abu al-Jarud who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Allah, the most Holy, the most High, will raise a people from their graves, on the Day of Judgment, their hands tied up to their necks and they will not be able to take anything even from a distance of one finger. Angels along with them censure them severely and say, “These were the ones who withheld a small amount of good, which could gain for them a great deal of good. These are those who refused to pay the right of Allah from their wealth.’”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/18)
text: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ قَرَنَ الزَّكَاةَ بِالصَّلَاةِ فَقَالَ أَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَ آتُوا الزَّكاةَ فَمَنْ أَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ وَ لَمْ يُؤْتِ الزَّكَاةَ لَمْ يُقِمِ الصَّلَاةَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Jamhour, from his father, from Ali Bin Hadeed, from Usman Bin Rusheyd, from Marouf bin Kharbouz,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Paired the Zakāt with the Salāt, so Heazwj Said [2:43] And establish the Salāt and give the Zakāt. Thus, the one who establishes the Salāt and does not give the Zakāt has not established the Salāt’.
en.sarwar: 23. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from ibn Jumhur from his father from Ali ibn Hadid from ‘Uthman from ibn Rashid from Ma‘ruf ibn Kharrabuz who has said the following: “Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, has joined zakat with Salah (prayer), saying, “Maintain Salah (prayer) and pay zakat.” Thus, if one does not pay zakat, he has not performed Salah (prayer).”’
